page_content:
KNES658 Teaching Internship in Physical Education (1-6 Credits) The MCERT PE internship is an intense experience that immerses interns into the life of their assigned Secondary school. The internship experience is designed to provide our pre-service teachers with a rich opportunity to learn the craft of teaching through close observation of experts as well as practice in the planning, delivery and assessment of instruction. Restriction: Must be in the Pre-K to 12 Physical Education Master of Education program. Repeatable to: 6 credits. Credit Only Granted for: KNES658 or KNES689T. Formerly: KNES689T.

KNES622 Teaching Secondary Physical Education (3 Credits) Students will learn aspects of teaching secondary physical education including: characteristics of secondary school students, testing, grading, teaching lifetime activities, and effective teaching techniques using SHAPE America Standards. Restriction: Must be enrolled in PreK-12 Physical Education Master of Education program. Credit Only Granted for: KNES689I OR KNES622. Formerly: KNES689I.

KNES628 PCS Research & Writing Workshop (3 Credits) This directed and project focused writing workshop provides students in Physical Cultural Studies, and related ﬁelds, the opportunity to develop a processual understanding of the writing craft, focused on enhancing academic writing and editing skills, practices, and routines. Repeatable to: 6 credits. Credit Only Granted for: KNES628 or KNES789P. Formerly: KNES789P.
